rationale: Establishes the internal security organization and management oversight.
Defines the specialized roles and responsibilities for cloud security.
Identifies and manages interfaces with external groups and authorities.
Ensures independent review and segregation of duties within the ISMS.
Maintains communication with special interest groups and security forums.
Mandates the annual review of information security policies.
Governs the dissemination of security instructions to all relevant parties.
Manages the lifecycle of project-specific security requirements.
Operationalizes the risk assessment process for cloud services.
Integrates supply chain risks into the overarching risk management plan.
Specifies the risk treatment plan for identified multi-tenant vulnerabilities.
Mandates disclosure of the primary jurisdiction and data center locations.
Discloses the location of administrative and support services.
Governs transparency regarding government requests for data.
Provides the service description and instructions for data portability.
Governs the user account management policy.
Ensures unique identification of all users and services.
Manages the provisioning and de-provisioning of user access.
Enforces the use of individualized accounts over shared accounts.
Implements regular review of access rights.
Enforces authentication strength (complexity and length).
Manages secret authentication information (password rotation/policy).
Governs the secure use of authentication information.
Mandates Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all console access.
Controls access to utility programs and sensitive system files.
Restricts access to system source code.
Manages privileged access rights (Privileged Access Management).
Restricts access to technical management interfaces.
Governs authentication for remote access via public networks.
Enforces session timeouts and inactive account disablement (90 days).
Secures the data center physical perimeter and entry points.
Implements physical protection against natural disasters.
Protects equipment from physical threats and environmental hazards.
Governs the visitor access policy and entry logs for sites.
Secures power and utility systems from interruptions.
Maintains the technical inventory of all information assets.
Assigns owners to all identified information assets.
Implements classification labeling for all data assets.
Governs the secure software development lifecycle policy.
Enforces change management for systems and applications.
Restricts changes to software packages and libraries.
Implements secure coding guidelines (e.g. OWASP).
Enforces security testing during the development process.
Governs the use of third-party components (SCA).
Secures the development environment and CI/CD pipeline.
Manages outsourced software development security.
Ensures integrity of the cloud service delivery platform.
Implements secure configuration and hardening benchmarks.
Governs the security of the virtualization/container layer.
Governs the policy for the use of cryptographic controls.
Enforces encryption of data in transit (TLS 1.2+).
Enforces encryption of data at rest (AES-256).
Manages the lifecycle of cryptographic keys (generation to destruction).
Governs the operational procedures for system health.
Implements malware protection on all cloud systems.
Manages the update and patch management process.
Ensures monitoring of system resources and capacity.
Mandates generation and protection of audit logs.
Ensures clock synchronization across the cloud environment.
Enforces regular review of logs for suspicious activity.
Protects logging facilities from unauthorized access.
Manages the monitoring of service availability.
Governs the security requirements for third-party suppliers.
Manages the security of the supplier supply chain.
Enforces regular monitoring of supplier service performance.
Governs the management of changes to supplier services.
Establishes the security incident management policy.
Governs the reporting of security events and weaknesses.
Manages the response to security incidents.
Ensures learning and improvement from past incidents.
Governs the collection of forensic evidence.
Defines the data classification categories (Confidentiality).
Governs the vetting of critical third-party software.
Operationalizes the internal audit of the ISMS.
Enforces periodic vulnerability scanning of the cloud stack.
Mandates annual external penetration testing.
Establishes the business continuity and disaster recovery plan.
Enforces regular testing of recovery procedures.
Governs the backup and restoration of data.
Ensures georedundancy of backup copies.
Manages responses to government investigation requests.
Ensures secure sanitization of storage media.
